text: breast-feeding in addition to nutrient-rich complementary foods (weaning foods), preferably up to 2 years. /c63Breast-feed the infant frequently and on demand to establish and maintain good milk supply. /c63Take a nutritionally adequate diet both during pregnancy and lactation. /c63Avoid tobacco (smoking and chewing), alcohol and drugs during lactation. /c63Ensure active family support for breast-feeding. 28 POINTS TO PONDER Guideline 4 What are supplementary/complementary foods? Why use complementary foods and when? Feed home based semi-solid foods to the infant after six months Rationale: /c69Breast-milk alone is not adequate for the infant beyond 6 months of age. /c69Introduction of food supplements (semi-solid complementary foods) along with breast-feeding is necessary for infants
